description: Baseline: total 3.12s, fields_merge dominates at 3.11s (99.8%)
Deduplicate fields by signature in find_conflicts_within — 143x speedup (3.12s → 21.7ms)
Replace Array#& with intersect? in mutually_exclusive?, optimize compared_fragments_key
Fast path for all-same-signature fields: skip group_by when all fields identical
New baseline with visibility profiles enabled — realistic workloads: 4.5ms total
Cache fields_and_fragments_from_selection — incorrect, parents affect mutually_exclusive checks
Eliminate redundant field lookups in FieldsAreDefinedOnType, deduplicate arguments call in RequiredArgumentsArePresent
Flatten fragment spreads into single field map — eliminates O(n²) recursive fragment cross-comparison. big_query -23%
Cache mutually_exclusive? type pair results, reduce array allocations in collect_fields_inner
Inline group_by into collect_fields_inner, skip on_field for leaf nodes — within noise

name: Optimizing graphql-ruby static validation (YJIT, visibility profiles)

description: Baseline with YJIT enabled — 2.7ms total (big_query 1.57ms)
Inline group_by into collect_fields_inner, skip on_field for leaf fields — big_query ~1.47ms

name: Optimizing graphql-ruby static validation (YJIT, visibility profiles, real-world query)

description: Baseline with real-world checkout schema + large_query (~20ms). Primary metric now includes large_query.
Match production config: did_you_mean(nil), allow_legacy_invalid_return_type_conflicts(false). large_query 15.4ms
Skip all_types loading + did_you_mean dictionary computation when schema.did_you_mean is nil. large_query 9.6ms (was 15.4ms, -37%)
Optimize add_conflict identity check (avoid expensive AST node #==). large_query 8.95ms. Verified no regression on smaller benchmarks.
Local field def cache in collect_fields_inner — double-caching over Profile, within noise
Cache collect_fields results in find_conflicts_between_sub_selection_sets — avoids re-expanding same sub-selections. large_query ~6.2ms (was ~9ms)
Fast path in FieldsHaveAppropriateSelections for common case (non-nil, non-leaf type with selections). Avoids kind/leaf checks on hot path.
Avoid forwardable delegation overhead: use @object_types directly in FragmentSpreadsArePossible and FragmentsAreOnCompositeTypes. Eliminates redundant types.type() lookups.
Eliminate redundant type lookups: FragmentTypesExist uses @object_types.last instead of @types.type(). Cache max_errors in FieldsWillMerge to avoid delegation.
Cache return_type and unwrapped_return_type on Field struct — avoids repeated Schema::Field#type and Wrapper#unwrap calls in find_conflict and find_conflicts_between_sub_selection_sets

name: Optimizing graphql-ruby static validation (pure validation, no Query/Profile creation)

description: Cache complete Profile#field result per (owner, field_name) — eliminates repeated kind checks, parent lookups, and visibility checks on warm cache. Universal win across all workloads.
Cache Profile#type result per type_name — eliminates repeated get_type + visibility + referenced? checks. All workloads improve.
Cache Wrapper#unwrap result — type wrappers are schema-level objects so this benefits all queries permanently. Eliminates recursive unwrap on repeated field visits.
Inline push_type into on_field/on_operation_definition/on_fragment_with_type — removes method indirection, simplifies code
Cache inline fragment path strings per type name — deduplicates ~500 string allocations per validation for queries with many inline fragments
Cache to_type_signature on NonNull and List wrappers — schema-level memoization, eliminates repeated string building
No code change — verification run showing stable performance at ~4160µs

Fresh before/after: TOTAL realistic 7650→3562µs (-53%), large_query 5991→2641µs (-56%), big_query 1477→780µs (-47%), fields_merge 1.8s→1.8ms (-99.9%). Higher iteration counts + 3-trial median for accuracy.
Baseline verification after context resume — stable at ~4100-4200µs on autoresearch.sh
Memoize Schema::Field#type for resolver_class path — breaks test because resolver class can be mutated after field creation; type must reflect latest state
Skip empty iteration in visitor: guard arguments/directives/selections with empty? check before iterating. Avoids ~6000 empty .each calls per validation in large_query.
Cache field_definition.type.unwrap per Schema::Field in visitor — avoids repeated type+unwrap dispatch chain. Bigger win for resolver-class schemas where Field#type isn't memoized.

Replace Field Struct with plain class — plain class initialization is ~1.7x faster than Struct.new with YJIT. Saves ~2600 struct allocations per validation. fields_merge improves most (5000 Field objects).

Reduce method dispatch overhead: use @types directly instead of context.types/context.query.types in FieldsWillMerge, RequiredArgumentsArePresent, VariablesAreInputTypes. Cache context.fragments in @fragments.
Cache required argument names per field definition in RequiredArgumentsArePresent — avoids re-iterating arguments for the same definition across field instances
Replace @field_definitions stack with @current_field_definition variable — eliminates push/pop per field visit (2247+ per validation). Uses simple variable assignment instead of array operations.
Replace @directive_definitions stack with @current_directive_definition variable — same pattern as field_definitions, eliminates push/pop for directives
Remove dead @directives array initialization

Replace @argument_definitions stack with @current/@parent_argument_definition variables — uses Ruby call stack for save/restore instead of Array push/pop. Handles arbitrary nesting depth correctly. Matters for argument-heavy queries.
Inline setting_errors block into on_field and on_operation_definition — eliminates 379 block closure allocations per validation

Skip FieldsWillMerge conflict check for fields whose direct selections are all unaliased, unique-named Fields with no fragments — avoids collect_fields+find_conflicts_within for ~55% of non-leaf fields
Inline field_definition accessor to @current_field_definition in RequiredArgumentsArePresent — avoids one method dispatch per field
Inline type_definition to @current_object_type in FieldsWillMerge on_field and on_operation_definition
Replace @path push/pop with indexed array + depth counter — pre-allocated Array.new(32) with @path_depth tracking eliminates Array#push/#pop overhead for ~5500 path operations per validation
Verification run — stable at ~3720-3880µs
Inline on_fragment_with_type into on_inline_fragment and on_fragment_definition — eliminates yield/block overhead and method dispatch for 531 fragment callbacks per validation
Final verification — stable at ~3740-3880µs on autoresearch.sh, ~3000µs on compare_validation.rb
Fix fast path in validate_field_selections: cover both common cases (leaf+no-selections ~70%, non-leaf+selections ~16%). Previous fast path only caught 16%. Now covers 86% of fields.
Cache selections_may_conflict? per selections array — hash lookup overhead exceeds computation savings, regression
Lazy visited_fragments hash in collect_fields — avoids hash alloc for fragment-free selection sets, but within noise on benchmarks
Cache single-element parent arrays per type + freeze + EMPTY_PARENTS constant — extra branching outweighs allocation savings, large_query regression
